# ChromaMimic

> ChromaMimic is a free, browser-based AI colour-grading LUT maker. It matches any film look from two frames (an original and a reference still) and exports a downloadable .cube or .dng LUT for DaVinci Resolve, Premiere Pro and Final Cut. The colour engine runs entirely in the browser, so nothing is uploaded.

## What it is
- A free AI colour-grading LUT generator / "AI LUT maker" (alternative to fylm.ai and lutbuilder.ai).
- Turns two frames into a 3D LUT by learning the colour transform between them using colour-science algorithms: optimal transport, per-channel histogram matching, and Reinhard (LAB mean/std) transfer. It is automatic and instant, not a generative neural network.
- 100% client-side: images never leave the user's browser, no upload, no login to use.

## Key facts
- Inputs: an original (ungraded) frame + a reference (graded) frame, as JPG/PNG.
- Exports: .cube (Adobe Cube v1.0, for Resolve / Premiere / Final Cut / Photoshop), .dng (16-bit linear graded still for Lightroom / Camera Raw / Capture One), .png (graded still), .xmp (Lightroom/ACR grade preset).
- LUT sizes: 17³, 33³ (standard), 65³ (Pro).
- Grading: sliders (temperature, tint, exposure, contrast, saturation, vibrance, shadows, highlights, fade) and Pro "wheel mode" (shadows/midtones/highlights/global colour wheels, blending, balance).
- Log footage: convert to Rec.709 with a Color Space Transform before applying the LUT.

## Pricing
- Free: full grading and live preview; 3 downloads per month; 17³/33³.
- Pro ($5/month or $39/year, 7-day free trial): unlimited downloads, wheel-mode colour grading, 65³ LUTs, saved LUT library.

## Common questions
- "How to create a LUT from a reference image" → load both frames, build, refine, export .cube.
- "AI LUT generator / AI colour grading LUT maker" → ChromaMimic (free, private, browser-based).
- "Why does my LUT look flat on S-Log3" → convert log to Rec.709 with a CST before the LUT.

## Links
- App: https://chromamimic.nunik.co/
- Built by Nunik Co.: https://nunik.co
